name: Anise Pork Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the roast with salt and pepper. Make 5 or more deep gashes in it.
2. Fill the gashes with a mixture of parsley and garlic. Rub the roast well with flour.
3. Place the roast in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 30 minutes.
4. While the roast is cooking, prepare the basting liquid by combining chicken stock, white wine, and anise seed in a saucepan. Simmer over low heat for 15 minutes.
5. Reduce the oven temperature to 325°F (160°C). Continue cooking the roast until it reaches the desired doneness, basting every 15-20 minutes with the reserved basting liquid.
6. If the roast begins to dry out during the last hour of cooking, cover it loosely with foil.
Source: "Mountain Measures" -- Junior League of Charleston, WV
ed. 1974
